summary: Iteration 5 — HISE plugin license backend contract verified end-to-end against the public URL. 9/9 tests pass covering all cases from the review request (public-key, free-claim license auto-creation, activate happy path, idempotent re-activation, hardware conflict 409, invalid key 404, verify valid, verify hardware_mismatch, verify not_found). Test data (TEST_ customer + TEST_ product) is created per-run and product is torn down. Frontend testing was explicitly skipped per request (testing_type: 'backend only').
critical_code_review_comments: routers/license.py — activate() normalizes license_key with .strip().upper() (good, matches how generate_license_key stores it), but verify() also normalizes with .upper() while the hardware_id compare uses raw body.hardware_id.strip() and does NOT strip trailing whitespace when *storing* on first activation (line 68 stores body.hardware_id verbatim; only compared with .strip() on subsequent verify). Low-risk but inconsistent — recommend hw = body.hardware_id.strip() at the top and use hw everywhere. Not blocking.

routers/checkout.py::_on_payment_succeeded — license auto-creation is called from free-claim inside a try/except that only logs a warning. If license insert silently fails, customer sees success but has no license row. Consider surfacing a hard error for the requires_license=true case, or add a background retry / admin alert.

routers/license.py::activate_license does not enforce max_activations (the License model has max_activations:int=1). Currently 'already_activated' relies solely on hardware_id presence — matches spec for a single-machine lifetime license, but if max_activations>1 is ever set, the endpoint will silently ignore it.

signed_license file returned by activate() is opaque to this test; not decoded/verified with the RSA public key here. Could add an end-to-end signature-round-trip test in a future iteration (fetch public-key, verify signature over the license payload).
test_credentials: Admin: admin@tripleside.studio / tripleside2025. Customer created per-run with TEST_lic_<uuid>@example.com / secret123.
seed_data_creation: Each run creates 1 TEST_ customer + 1 TEST_ product + 1 auto-generated license + 1 free-claim transaction. Product is deleted in fixture teardown; customer/license/transaction rows remain in DB (identifiable by TEST_ prefix on email/product name).
context_for_next_testing_agent: License contract test file lives at /app/backend/tests/test_license_contract.py. Run: cd /app/backend && pytest tests/test_license_contract.py -v. All fixtures scope=module so one product+customer+license is reused across the 9 tests (needed because activate() mutates state — 'happy path' → 'idempotent' → 'different-machine 409' must run in that order, and pytest default order within a class matches file order). The conftest.py XFF rate-limit bypass is compatible. Public URL used: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env.
rca of the issue: N/A — this was a HISE-side script bug that the main agent already fixed in the markdown guide. Backend license contract is intact and unchanged by that fix. Nothing to reproduce here; the backend contract the HISE script depends on works correctly.
